description: Canonical service onboarding procedures for already-registered DevGov services. Machine-local paths and transient probe evidence stay in reports/.

entries.healthProcedure: Use /health on the governed dashboard origin.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use package.json#scripts.doctor.
entries.resetProcedure: Use doctor:repair only for regenerating local reports; dashboard restart/reset execution remains separate from Doctor.
entries.startupProcedure: Use scripts/open-dashboard.mjs for on-demand start and startup registry entries for reviewed login startup.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status row is already surfaced as devgov-dashboard plus public-route rows.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the DevGov public route registry and reviewed tunnel startup scripts for gov/dev hostnames.
entries.nextAction: Keep as reference implementation for the remaining service onboarding records.
entries.notes: This is the completed baseline service-control pattern.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:3201/health on the loopback-only service-control listener.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov service-control doctor wrapper to validate listener health and canonical registry shape.
entries.resetProcedure: Keep listener restart review-gated because the service-control listener is the executor; use dashboard startup/restart authority only after separate review.
entries.startupProcedure: Start with the reviewed DevGov dashboard process through scripts/open-dashboard.mjs or the existing dashboard startup registration path.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface as DevGov Service Control in Network Service Status with Health, Doctor, and review-gated Reset state.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Never publish the service-control listener through Cloudflare. Browser origins may call it only from reviewed DevGov dashboard origins.
entries.nextAction: Design a separate governance control panel before approving any self-restart or registry-edit action from the dashboard.
entries.notes: This row is the self-governance baseline for DevGov control-plane health. Canonical data does not store process IDs, command lines, or event logs.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the registered loopback /health endpoint.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate Windows service state, token presence, and the authenticated /health endpoint.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov restart wrapper to restart the native Windows service and wait for authenticated /health recovery without exposing token values.
entries.startupProcedure: Keep restart review-gated through local-archive-maintainer/scripts/install-app-server-service.ps1.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status local-agent row uses approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public Cloudflare route is registered; keep loopback-only unless a future route review is approved.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether the unauthenticated quick-test row should be normalized around the protected 401 response or replaced with a separate operator-safe health contract.
entries.notes: Bearer token paths, service home, logs, and generated indexes must remain out of canonical registry data.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the project Doctor in read-only mode to confirm PS3 Eye USB presence, DirectShow input availability, frame freshness, virtual-camera registration, and capture viability.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to run the stable PS3 Eye virtual-camera checks and summarize failing sub-checks.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov restart wrapper to run the bounded PS3 Eye repair/start path and then re-run doctor checks.
entries.startupProcedure: Default policy is on-demand and login startup disabled; keep ps3eye-system-camera-login as a candidate only for reviewed opt-in service mode.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface PS3 Eye as a local observation target with approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route applies; camera observation must stay local-only and must not expose images or device streams through public routes.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether PS3 Eye should gain a separate quick-test health contract beyond the current doctor-first observation model.
entries.notes: The current PS3 Eye bridge needs long-lived ffmpeg/feeder state when enabled; do not register it as an always-on service for Codex observation without a separate privacy and resource review.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the registered public /api/health route plus the runtime local health target written by scripts/runtime/windows-persistent-server.ps1.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the DevGov Doctor wrapper to check the runtime status script, loopback 127.0.0.1:4321 health, and public staging /api/health together.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov restart wrapper to ensure the local runtime is healthy on 127.0.0.1:4321, while keeping any public route mismatch visible until the tunnel target is fixed.
entries.startupProcedure: Keep startup review-gated through codex-calendar-todo/scripts/windows-persistent-server.ps1 and runtime scripts.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the registered staging-private route; confirm Access and runtime health together before approval.
entries.nextAction: Decide whether the staging-private route still needs stronger Cloudflare Access enforcement evidence and whether tunnel startup should be promoted into a dedicated reviewed authority.
entries.notes: The startup target is a runtime project; do not point login startup at the development worktree. The public route and local runtime now align on 127.0.0.1:4321.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the verified local and public /healthz endpoints, plus /readyz for readiness confirmation.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov codex-remote doctor wrapper to run the existing runtime status scripts and confirm local/public healthz and readyz.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov codex-remote restart wrapper, which runs the existing on-login bootstrap and keeps local/public checks visible.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the existing reviewed startup authority at codex/Start-CodexRemoteOnLogin.ps1 through the local runtime home.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the dedicated Cloudflare Tunnel route for codex-remote.colorgeek.co; validate ingress and Access posture before probing public /healthz and /readyz.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether startup persistence evidence should be promoted into a separate DevGov status row, distinct from on-demand recovery.
entries.notes: The durable owner is the runtime home under Documents/Codex rather than a normal source repository.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:3189/health endpoint plus the public TB2 prod/staging /health routes.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov TB2 doctor wrapper to run status-runtime, verify prod/staging runtime state, and compare local/public health results together.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov TB2 restart wrapper, which runs the Windows start-local-mcp authority and then keeps staging drift or public-route failures visible.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed Windows entrypoint scripts and the TB2 local MCP start authority; login startup remains outside the current DevGov control surface.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route rows use approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the approved public-health Cloudflare route records; do not expose MCP execution through public health routes.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether the active TB2 tunnel startup should be promoted into a dedicated reviewed startup authority instead of remaining an operator-managed background connector.
entries.notes: The Windows entrypoint keeps source and runtime boundaries separate. DevGov control now confirms local health, public prod/staging health, and prod/staging runtime verify together.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local /health on the governed 127.0.0.1:43189 origin plus the public MCP metadata and discovery probe.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to run production status checks and confirm local/public /health for the protected MCP boundary without exposing credential values.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed project start-windows-production path; it must require the dedicated local-files Cloudflare config or a reviewed override before starting tunnel transport.
entries.startupProcedure: Keep startup as a candidate through registry/startup.registry.json#chatgpt-local-files-mcp-windows-runtime until normal ChatGPT Web protected OAuth write acceptance passes.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Doctor through the local-only DevGov control listener; Restart remains review-gated until protected OAuth acceptance evidence is current.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Route mcp.colorgeek.co only to the ChatGPT-facing local-files MCP safety boundary. Do not route ChatGPT directly to the TB2 runtime or the Nowledge browser bridge.
entries.nextAction: Complete normal signed-in ChatGPT Web connector acceptance before promoting a dashboard-safe Restart action for this protected public boundary.
entries.notes: This record resolves the previous mcp.colorgeek.co drift by making chatgpt-local-files-mcp the registered ChatGPT-facing safety boundary while keeping TB2 behind a narrow probe.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:14242/health after scripts/start-nowledge-compat.ps1 starts the runtime.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to run Nowledge compatibility tests and report local /health state without reading token values.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which invokes scripts/start-nowledge-compat.ps1 and confirms the loopback /health contract.
entries.startupProcedure: No login startup is approved yet. Keep this on-demand until extension acceptance and data-egress checks are complete.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface as a local runtime-command service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Reset controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public route. Keep loopback-only; do not publish 127.0.0.1:14242 or browser-bridge websocket through Cloudflare.
entries.nextAction: Complete live Chrome extension acceptance against the local API before any public or browser-bridge exposure review.
entries.notes: This service is a local replacement boundary for Nowledge Memory Exchange API calls, not a browser automation bridge.

entries.healthProcedure: Use runtime:check and the registered public route health target.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use scripts/check-runtime.mjs as the stable runtime Doctor for local and public reachability.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov Taste restart wrapper to ensure the local Next.js runtime is listening, then keep any remaining tunnel or auth failures visible for follow-up.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov on-demand ensure-running wrapper for 127.0.0.1:3010; no login startup automation is registered yet.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Taste Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Keep the public route mapped to the governed loopback listener and align tunnel docs before changes.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether Taste also needs reviewed login startup automation beyond the current on-demand control path.
entries.notes: The public listener must remain governed by 127.0.0.1:3010 and the reviewed tunnel route.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the verified OpenAI-compatible /v1/models endpoint on local 127.0.0.1:1234 and the protected public route.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov LM Studio doctor wrapper to check vendor CLI server status plus local/public /v1/models reachability.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov LM Studio restart wrapper, which runs lms server start and keeps any remaining public-route failure visible.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the vendor-reviewed LM Studio CLI authority at lms server start for on-demand recovery; login startup remains an app-level choice outside DevGov.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Keep Cloudflare Access required and confirm whether this route should remain publicly reachable.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether LM Studio should also have reviewed login-startup expectations, distinct from the current on-demand API server control path.
entries.notes: Treat as a protected local model API. DevGov uses the vendor CLI control surface rather than a private reverse-engineered launcher path.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:4173/index.html endpoint after the static preview server is running.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate the governed port registration, static demo files, and live preview response.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which starts only the local static preview server and does not touch calibration state.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand DevGov wrapper for demo/display-shader-control-lab via python -m http.server; no login startup automation is registered.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface Display Shader Control Lab as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep this preview local-only.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether ShaderGlass launch/open-folder actions should become separate DevGov-managed controls distinct from preview health.
entries.notes: This is a preview service, not a calibration authority.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:3287/health endpoint described in SBS PORTS.md and local proxy docs.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use scripts/proxy-doctor.mjs as the stable SBS local proxy Doctor.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov SBS restart wrapper, which runs proxy-doctor in start mode and keeps any backend warnings visible.
entries.startupProcedure: Use scripts/start-proxy-protocol.ps1 and proxy:doctor:start as the reviewed on-demand ensure-running path; no login startup entry is required.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface SBS as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep the extension proxy loopback-only.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether LM Studio backend readiness should become a separate DevGov-managed local service row.
entries.notes: This service already has strong project-local proxy health documentation.

entries.healthProcedure: Run: tunnel-client health --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/readyz --json and confirm readiness.
entries.doctorProcedure: Run: openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/tunnel-client-runtime.ps1 -Mode Doctor. Doctor checks plugin-hint presence and profile mount path coverage.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: stop/restart or runtime override should be explicit before automation.
entries.startupProcedure: Run: openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/register-tunnel-client-startup.ps1 to enable startup; runtime start uses openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/tunnel-client-runtime.ps1 -Mode QuickStart.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Keep service-status row as candidate until health and doctor checks complete end-to-end.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public route. Keep local-only unless a reviewed public route is added.
entries.nextAction: Collect one completed health + doctor + startup evidence report and promote after validation.
entries.notes: MCP command mounts reviewed local workspace and model-library roots.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:3100/url-hero/ Vite dev entrypoint from the root url-hero worktree.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate the governed port plus the root url-hero lint and test scripts.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which starts only the root url-hero Vite dev server and does not modify the dirty target repo.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand DevGov wrapper for npm run dev from the root url-hero worktree; no login startup automation is registered.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface URL Hero as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved for the dev server; keep local-only.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether the nested duplicate url-hero/ folder should stay as evidence-only or be retired after a separate source-of-truth cleanup.
entries.notes: The root url-hero worktree is the active source authority for this control path; DevGov does not modify the pre-existing dirty target repo.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats endpoint on the localhost-only ComfyUI runtime.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ComfyUI doctor wrapper to validate the portable runtime layout, model links, process presence, and localhost health endpoint.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ComfyUI restart wrapper to ensure the portable runtime is running on 127.0.0.1:8188 without widening exposure beyond localhost.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand ComfyUI local start path in the target repo; login startup is not registered in DevGov.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface ComfyUI as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep ComfyUI loopback-only unless a later exposure review explicitly changes that boundary.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether ComfyUI should gain a reviewed login-startup authority, distinct from the current on-demand localhost control path.
entries.notes: The target repo is an operations workspace, not the upstream ComfyUI source tree; DevGov reuses the repo-level localhost-only service-control scripts rather than editing the dirty worktree.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/health endpoint exposed by `photo_hdr_flow web`.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov Photo HDR Flow doctor wrapper to run `.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m photo_hdr_flow doctor --json` from the source checkout.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which starts the loopback-only `photo_hdr_flow web --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765` server and waits for `/api/health`.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand web UI start path from the source checkout; no login startup automation is registered in DevGov.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface Photo HDR Flow as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep the web UI and local API loopback-only.
entries.nextAction: Archive stale references in historical notes where `video-render-kit` is used as a placeholder; canonical project authority remains `photo-hdr-flow`.
entries.notes: The former `video-render-kit` 8765 placeholder resolved to the Photo HDR Flow source checkout. DevGov reuses the reviewed loopback web UI entrypoint without editing the pre-existing target repo.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/state and confirm ffmpegExists and ffprobeExists are both true.
entries.doctorProcedure: Run the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to check required binaries, server script presence, and `/api/state` runtime state.
entries.resetProcedure: Run the reviewed DevGov restart wrapper on `video-render-kit` and confirm that `/api/state` becomes healthy.
entries.startupProcedure: Start from the reviewed portable source checkout using `start-control-panel.ps1`; login startup is not registered in DevGov.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface as a local service target with approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep loopback-only unless a later exposure review explicitly changes that boundary.
entries.nextAction: Keep port references aligned with canonical 5001 governance entries; remove remaining 8765 references outside historical logs.
entries.notes: Portable FFmpeg control panel. Run only loopback. DevGov does not store machine-local evidence such as transient process logs or old source-only paths.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:3102/healthz and confirm the Review Studio shell plus runtime admin settings route load.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate governed port registration, server entry, lint, tests, and docs sync checks.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which builds and starts the loopback-only production server with PORT=3102, SKILL0_HOST=127.0.0.1, and SKILL0_MODE=standalone.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand DevGov wrapper; no login startup automation is registered in DevGov.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Reset controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep the Review Studio interface loopback-only unless a later exposure review explicitly changes that boundary.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether canonical parser bridge availability should become a separate DevGov-managed readiness dimension.
entries.notes: Review-first Skill-0 workbench with optional runtime LLM settings. API keys and transient runtime overrides must not be stored in canonical registry data.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:31700/ and confirm the draw-draw page title or shell content loads from the loopback Vite server.
entries.doctorProcedure: Run the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate the governed port registration, draw-draw source shape, workspace names, and live root response when online.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which starts only the loopback draw-draw Vite server through `npm run dev` and strictPort.
entries.startupProcedure: No login startup is approved. Personal launcher tooling may locate or start the local app, but it remains outside canonical registry data.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface draw-draw as a local service target with approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public route. Keep loopback-only; do not publish 127.0.0.1:31700 without separate exposure review.
entries.nextAction: Keep the personal workflow wrapper local-only and update it separately if the cached plugin launcher falls behind the draw-draw workspace name.
entries.notes: Local UI review canvas for screenshot annotation. The personal workflow wrapper is intentionally not recorded in canonical registry data.

entries.healthProcedure: Use local http://127.0.0.1:3101/ and confirm the Vite shell responds from the loopback-only dev server.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov doctor wrapper to validate the governed port registration plus GSDF/EOTF lint and regression tests.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov ensure-running wrapper, which starts only the loopback Vite dev server through `npm run dev` with strictPort.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed on-demand DevGov wrapper for npm run dev; no login startup automation is registered.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface GSDF/EOTF as a local Vite runtime with approved Health, Doctor, and Reset controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public route is approved; keep the dev server loopback-only.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether extension smoke testing should become a separate DevGov-managed action distinct from local Vite runtime health.
entries.notes: Local development UI for the GSDF/EOTF video adjuster. DevGov does not modify the target project during startup checks.
